# haven — Code of Conduct

All participants in the haven project are expected to follow this Code of Conduct. Its purpose is to ensure a welcoming, inclusive, and respectful community.

## Our standards

Be respectful and professional. Examples of expected behavior:

- Be considerate, kind, and patient.
- Use welcoming and inclusive language.
- Assume good faith in others’ intentions.
- Give constructive feedback and acknowledge contributions.

Unacceptable behavior includes, but is not limited to:

- Harassment, hate speech, or insults.
- Threats, doxxing, or sharing private information.
- Discriminatory language or exclusionary conduct.
- Sustained disruption of discussions or project activities.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to all project spaces (issue trackers, pull requests, discussions, chat channels, mailing lists, events, and other project-related interactions).

## Reporting

If you experience or witness a violation, please report it to the project maintainers:

- Email: webmaster at huybrechts dot xyz
- Or open a private issue or use the repository’s reporting mechanisms if available.

Include as much relevant information as possible (what happened, when, and links or screenshots if applicable). Reports will be handled confidentially to the extent possible.

## Enforcement

Maintainers will review reports and determine appropriate actions, which may include:

- Private warnings
- Temporary or permanent removal of contributor privileges
- Public statements or further remediation as needed

Decisions about enforcement will be made by maintainers and may involve other community members when appropriate. Appeals can be directed to the same contact address.

## Attribution

This policy is adapted from common open source community guidelines. Projects are encouraged to customize reporting contacts and enforcement details to match their governance.
